purpose.summary: COPAUA0C is a CICS program that processes authorization requests from a message queue. It retrieves requests, processes them, and then sends a response. The program interacts with IMS to schedule and terminate PSBs.
purpose.business_context: This program likely serves as part of a larger transaction processing system, handling authorization requests for financial transactions.

inputs.name: MQTM
inputs.description: Message Queue Trigger Monitor data containing the request queue name and trigger data.

inputs.name: REQUEST-QUEUE
inputs.description: The WebSphere MQ queue from which authorization requests are read.

outputs.name: RESPONSE-QUEUE
outputs.description: The WebSphere MQ queue to which authorization responses are written.

outputs.name: IMS
outputs.description: Updates to IMS database segments related to authorization processing.

called_programs.purpose: Reads a message from the request message queue.
Writes a message to the response message queue.
Closes the request and response message queues.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program entry point. It appears to call several subroutines (CMQODV, CMQMDV, CMQV, etc.) but their purpose cannot be determined from the provided code. The program then proceeds to the MAIN-PARA to execute the core logic.

This paragraph orchestrates the main processing flow of the program. It first calls 1000-INITIALIZE to perform initial setup, including retrieving trigger monitor data and opening the request queue. Then, it calls 2000-MAIN-PROCESS to process authorization requests from the queue. Finally, it calls 9000-TERMINATE to close the queues and perform cleanup before returning to CICS. The paragraph uses EXEC CICS RETURN to terminate the CICS transaction.

This paragraph performs initialization tasks for the program. It retrieves the queue name and trigger data from the CICS RETRIEVE command, storing them in WS-REQUEST-QNAME and WS-TRIGGER-DATA respectively. It sets WS-WAIT-INTERVAL to 5000, likely representing a wait time in milliseconds. It then calls 1100-OPEN-REQUEST-QUEUE to open the request queue and 3100-READ-REQUEST-MQ to read the first request message from the queue. The paragraph handles the case where the CICS RETRIEVE command fails by checking EIBRESP, but takes no specific action.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions.

This paragraph opens the request message queue. It moves MQOT-Q to MQOD-OBJECTTYPE and WS-REQUEST-QNAME to MQOD-OBJECTNAME, configuring the object descriptor for the MQOPEN call. It computes WS-OPTIONS as MQOO-INPUT-SHARED, indicating shared access to the queue. It then calls MQOPEN to open the queue, passing the connection handle, object descriptor, options, object handle, completion code, and reason code. If the MQOPEN call is successful (WS-COMPCODE = MQCC-OK), it sets WS-REQUEST-MQ-OPEN to TRUE. Otherwise, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR, setting ERR-LOCATION to 'M001', ERR-CRITICAL and ERR-MQ to TRUE, and populating ERR-CODE-1, ERR-CODE-2, and ERR-MESSAGE with relevant error information.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 1100-OPEN-REQUEST-QUEUE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions.

This paragraph schedules a PSB (Program Specification Block) in IMS. It executes a DLI SCHD command with the PSB-NAME. The DIBSTAT is moved to IMS-RETURN-CODE. If the PSB was scheduled more than once, the paragraph terminates the PSB and schedules it again. If the status is OK, IMS-PSB-SCHD is set to TRUE. Otherwise, it logs an error using 9500-LOG-ERROR, setting ERR-LOCATION to 'I001', ERR-CRITICAL and ERR-IMS to TRUE, and populating ERR-CODE-1 and ERR-MESSAGE with relevant error information.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 1200-SCHEDULE-PSB paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions.

This paragraph processes authorization requests in a loop. The loop continues until NO-MORE-MSG-AVAILABLE or WS-LOOP-END is set to TRUE. Inside the loop, it calls 2100-EXTRACT-REQUEST-MSG to extract the request message, then calls 5000-PROCESS-AUTH to process the authorization. It increments WS-MSG-PROCESSED and issues a CICS SYNCPOINT. It then sets IMS-PSB-NOT-SCHD to TRUE. If WS-MSG-PROCESSED exceeds WS-REQSTS-PROCESS-LIMIT, WS-LOOP-END is set to TRUE. Otherwise, it calls 3100-READ-REQUEST-MQ to read the next request message. The paragraph manages the overall flow of processing multiple authorization requests.
This paragraph serves as the exit point for the 2000-MAIN-PROCESS paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement and does not perform any specific actions.

error_handling.action: Logs an error and sets error flags.
